chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, LARKANA Crl.Revision Appln.No.S-101 of2021. _____________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF HON’BLE JUDGE ________________________________________________________ 01. For orders on M.A.No.9037/2021 (U/A). 02. For orders on office objection “A”. 03. For orders on M.A.No.9038/2021 (E/A). 04. For hearing of main case. 03.01.2022 Mr. Faraz Ali Bijarani, Advocate for applicant. = * = * = * = * = * = 1. Urgency Granted. 2 to 4. Facts in brief necessary for disposal of instant Crl.Revision Application are that the applicant filed a direct complaint u/s.3/4/5 of Illegal Dispossession Act, 2005, it was dismissed by learned Sessions Judge, Kashmore @ Kandhkot, vide order dated 12.11.2021, which is impugned by the applicant before this Court by preferring the instant Crl.Revision Application. It is contended by learned counsel for the applicant that the applicant is lawful purchaser of the subject house by way of sale agreement; therefore, the learned trial Court ought not to have dismissed his complaint by way of impugned order; such order being illegal is liable to be set aside with direction to learned trial Court to take cognizance of the incident. I have considered the above arguments and perused the record. The applicant is making claim over the subject house on the basis of sale agreement, which is yet to be enforced. The proposed accused as per reports furnished by SHO, P.S, A-Section Kandhkot and Mukhtiarkar (Revenue) Taluka Kandhkot, are in possession of the subject house since twenty years. Apparently, no dispossession of the applicant from the subject house at the hands of the proposed accused has taken place; in these circumstances, the learned trial Court was right to dismiss the complaint of the applicant by way of impugned order, which is not found to be illegal to be interfered with by this Court by way of instant Crl.Revision Application; same fails and is dismissed in limine.

The proposed accused as per reports furnished by SHO, P.S, A-Section Kandhkot and Mukhtiarkar (Revenue) Taluka Kandhkot, are in possession of the subject house since twenty years. Apparently, no dispossession of the applicant from the subject house at the hands of the proposed accused has taken place; in these circumstances, the learned trial Court was right to dismiss the complaint of the applicant by way of impugned order, which is not found to be illegal to be interfered with by this Court by way of instant Crl.Revision Application; same fails and is dismissed in limine. J U D G E
